collect_dependency_scanning_alerts.documentation: Use this option to collect dependency scanning findings from GitLab projects. For more information, see [GitLab dependency scanning](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/application_security/dependency_scanning/).

collect_container_scanning_alerts.documentation: Use this option to collect container scanning findings from GitLab projects. For more information, see [GitLab container scanning](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/application_security/container_scanning/).

collect_audit_events.documentation: Use this option to collect GitLab audit events. For more information, see [GitLab audit events](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/compliance/audit_events/).

use_credential_vault.displayName: Use credential vault for Dynatrace authentication

credential_vault_id.displayName: Select vault credential for Dynatrace authentication

token.description: Token used to ingest GitLab security events into Dynatrace.
token.documentation: For more information, see [Generate an access token](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/manage/identity-access-management/access-tokens-and-oauth-clients/access-tokens#create-api-token).

metadata.placeholder: Provide an access token with the openpipeline.events_security scope

use_custom_security_ingest_url.displayName: Use custom security events ingest endpoint

dynatrace_security_ingest_url.documentation: For example: `https://<yourEnvironmentID>.live.dynatrace.com/platform/ingest/v1/security.events`. For more information, see [Security events ingest](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/secure/threat-observability/security-events-ingest).

url.description: The base URL where the GitLab API is reachable.
url.documentation: Use `https://gitlab.com` for GitLab.com. For self-managed GitLab, provide your server base URL.

token.description: Personal Access Token used to authenticate to GitLab APIs.

metadata.placeholder: Provide a GitLab PAT with API access

all_groups.documentation: If enabled, security findings and scans will be collected from all accessible GitLab groups. If disabled, only the groups listed below are used.

groups.documentation: Use a human-readable group value. Supported matching values are group name, group path, or full group path. Example: `platform-team` or `acme/platform-team`.

all_projects.documentation: If enabled, security findings and scans will be collected from all projects in the selected group scope. If disabled, only the projects listed below are used.

projects.documentation: Use a human-readable project value. Supported matching values are project name, project path, or project path with namespace. Example: `service-api` or `acme/platform/service-api`.

first_time_fetch_window_days.displayName: Security events initial fetch time window
first_time_fetch_window_days.description: How far back to look for security events during the initial run.

security_findings_frequency_hours.description: How often GitLab security findings are collected.

audit_event_frequency_minutes.description: The extension will fetch audit logs using the specified time period.

debug_logs.description: Enable detailed debug logging for troubleshooting.
